title: docker-compose-like syntax for podman play kube
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind feature

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

Users frequently want a simplified way to develop applications consisting of multiple services. For applications deployed into Kubernetes, users frequently use tools like kind, k3d, or minikube, to create a Kubernetes cluster into which to deploy their application. These tools introduce all the power, but also complexity, of Kubernetes.

`podman play kube` is analogous, in some ways, to `docker-compose`: both tools can start and stop groups of related containers, which frequently constitute parts of a microservice application. This technique for starting multiple containers as a group, defined in a single YAML configuration file, may be a suitable replacement for a full Kubernetes cluster for a subset of Kubernetes applications.

`kubectl` and `docker-compose` have a syntax that that is "isomorphic." For example, `kubectl create -f` and `kubectl delete -f` on the same file are effectively the inverse operations of each other. If you create resources using `kubectl create -f` and a manifest file, the inverse operation (`kubectl delete -f`) should delete everything as well. Similarly, `docker-compose` supports both `up` (running all containers) and `down` (stop all containers) commands. This similarity makes things easier for users to learn and use.

By contrast, `podman play kube` uses a special `--down` flag to perform the inverse operation, which might be confusing, as not all the same flag values are applicable to both. It also does not seem like tearing a deployment down is a special case of `play` operation, but rather, a separate operation entirely.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1.

2.

3.

**Describe the results you expected:**

```
$ podman version
Version:      3.4.1
API Version:  3.4.1
Go Version:   go1.17.2
Built:        Wed Dec 31 16:00:00 1969
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
(paste your output here)
```

```

```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes/No

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body: Please describe the syntax you would like?
Or better yet open a PR.

@rhatdan Thanks for triaging! I'd be happy to open a PR, and we can do it in a compatible way by adding the commands alongside the existing ones, as an alias. Perhaps something like:

```bash
# run the containers in the manifest file.yaml
podman kube up -f file.yaml
# run the containers in a manifest with a default name, like podman-kube.yaml
podman kube up
# then the inverse operations would destroy the pods
podman kube down
podman kube down -f file.yaml
```

It may be preferable to maintain the current hierarchy, in which case we could do `podman play kube up` etc.

As a separate item (#12476), we could consider adding `podman play compose up` to run a docker-compose.yaml file through kompose, and then do the same thing as kube play. In theory this would mean that the whole docker-compose ecosystem would become available for podman users.

Yes I would prefer podman play kube up and podman play kube down.
Isn't docker-compose (2) now a separate package?  Could podman play compose just use that and exec the docker-compose package?

Even better, it's a Docker CLI plugin - if we were to implement that API, we'd have it integrated natively.

We'd still need to resolve the other blockers for Compose v2.0 support first, of course.

Yes it should be possible to do this without breaking changes. You would need to create new cobra commands and then you just export the run function and the flag definitions and add them to the new commands.

I personally like `podman kube play` and `podman kube down`. Adding extra features such as the default filename can be added later.
I'm a little more in favor of keeping existing subcommand structure, so kmaybe `podman play kube` and `podman down kube` (or even a subcommand of `podman stop`? `podman stop kube`?)
`podman stop kube` is not possible, the cli parser cannot know if kube is a subcommand or the container name used for `podman stop`
podman down kube seems like a nice compromise.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

Moving [this comment](https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/13295#issuecomment-1046675512) from @vrothberg on the PR here since the rest of the discussion was happening in this issue. The proposed `podman kube [up|down|generate]` command syntax [was supported](https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/13295#issuecomment-1046980370) by @mheon:

> Independent of this PR. I think we should reflect on how to name the commands. I would love to have a `podman kube down` instead of `down kube` (similar to the container, image and pod sub-commands).
> 
> `play kube` and `generate kube` could then be aliases for `kube play/generate`.

I don't personally have any opinion and will defer to you all as the maintainers on the preferred implementation approach. Once we have more consensus on the preferred design, I'll update the PR accordingly. Since this will be part of Podman's API, changing it once merged will be difficult (i.e. this is a difficult-to-reverse decision), and it is thus important that we all agree on the approach.

Thanks everyone for your support getting this feature implemented!

We should have this discussion at a Cabal meeting.
podman generate kube| systemd (Makes sense to me.
But if we add a podman kube command, then I think we would need

podman kube generate|up|down
Would we want 
podman systemd generate?

@rhatdan Since there's been a flurry of review activity on the PR (thanks all!), I wanted to ask: do you want me to hold off on this work until the next Podman Community Cabal meeting? I suppose I just missed the meeting last Thursday (Feb 17) according to the [community meeting page](https://podman.io/community/meeting/), and presumably the next one will be around March 17th timeframe?

I can also speculatively just do `podman kube generate|up|down` and change it later if necessary, since it's not a huge amount of work to shuffle things around

> Would we want podman systemd generate?

I think that `generate kube/systemd` is fine since we are generating data. `generate kube` should alias to `kube generate` though, just to make sure all kube-things would live behind `podman kube ...`.
@jawnsy No I think you can go forward with your commands, I think we have come to consensus and these are better the podman play kube.
I agree. We can do the changes after your PR has merged.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
once we get done with bugfix mode, we should tackle this rework of the cobra CLI
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@nicrowe00 PTAL

with the `podman play` syntax we can add more ways to create pods/containers (e.g. we have a open PR like https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/14729).

We have:

- podman generate (kube|systemd)
- podman play (kube)

We could add a new command `podman down $KIND` and it will be cleaner than a `podman kube` specific subcommand
@giuseppe, we are more looking into consolidating all kube-related things into `podman kube` (see https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/12475#issuecomment-1047546589).
